title: Sursum Corda
content:
Seek not the spirit, if it hide
    Inexorable to thy zeal:
    Trembler, do not whine and chide:
    Art thou not also real?
    Stoop not then to poor excuse;
    Turn on the accuser roundly; say,
    'Here am I, here will I abide
    Forever to myself soothfast;
    Go thou, sweet Heaven, or at thy pleasure stay!'
    Already Heaven with thee its lot has cast,
    For only it can absolutely deal.
meaning:
"Sursum Corda" is a quintessential example of Ralph Waldo Emerson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
